caption_gen:
The epidermis shows mild atypia. The dermis contains a dense infiltrate of atypical melanocytes with irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli.

Melanoma.

The image shows a dermal proliferation of melanocytes. There is a prominent atypical melanocytic proliferation with irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli.

Melanoma.
Basal cell hyperplasia and increased melanin pigment are present. The diagnosis is MELANOMA.

Melanocytic proliferation with atypical features including irregular nuclear contours and pleomorphism.
Melanoma.
The epidermis shows a compound nevus pattern with nests of melanocytes and a background of junctional and compound nevi. The dermal component is unremarkable. Diagnosis: Compound nevus.

The image shows a dense proliferation of spindle cells with elongated nuclei and eosinophilic cytoplasm. There is a prominent inflammatory infiltrate. The spindle cells are arranged in fascicles.

Melanoma.

The epidermis shows a regular pattern of rete ridges and dermal papillae. There is a band of melanocytes at the dermoepidermal junction. There is a prominent band of melanin pigment in the dermis.
Melanoma.

The image shows a dermal proliferation of melanocytic nests. Some nests exhibit atypical nuclear features.
Melanoma.
The image shows a dermal nevus with a compound pattern. There are dermal melanocytes and dermal papillae. The diagnosis is Compound Nevus.

The epidermis shows a regular pattern of nesting melanocytes. There are scattered brown pigment globules. The dermis shows a dense lymphocytic infiltrate.
Melanoma.
